summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the mellow 7/26/72 show, highlighted by a stellar Dark Star → Comes A Time sequence and crisp, clear audio.
review: The recording is a superb sound‑board transfer that delivers crystal‑clear detail, letting Jerry’s velvet‑smooth vocals and Garcia’s soaring lead shine. Musically the night is relaxed and mellow, with the Dark Star → Comes A Time suite standing out alongside superb China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and a sweet He's Gone. A few listeners note minor harmony quirks and a missing fragment of Casey Jones, but overall the performance and sound are highly praised.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (≈90 %) award five stars and praise both the musical performance and the audio fidelity; only one outlier gives a low rating, so the combined show‑and‑recording quality is judged excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear and well‑balanced soundboard transfer; reviewers repeatedly call it “awesome” or “excellent”. A few notes mention slight missing bits (Casey Jones) and occasional weak harmonies, but no major drop‑outs.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical mid‑70s three‑song opening set followed by a relaxed, mellow second set; strong banter and smooth pacing make it an easy‑listening night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is described as “velvet moonbeam” and his guitar tone “incredible”; multiple reviewers highlight his vocal and lead work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted for “huge bombs” and a thunderous presence, especially during the Dark Star jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for driving rhythm (“fantastic rhythm driving the train”) and strong pre‑verse jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (and occasionally Brent) are singled out for standout playing in the pre‑verse jam; keyboards described as “delight”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann called a “machine” with thunderous power, especially on the Dark Star climax.
